  • Your first chat with Claude

You are an expert Android developer. Create a COMPLETE Android Studio project for a "Medicine Reminder App" with clean architecture, beginner-friendly code, and NO UI freezing or ANR (App Not Responding) issues. IMPORTANT REQUIREMENTS: - Use Kotlin only - Use XML layouts (NOT Jetpack Compose) - Use MVVM architecture (simple version) - Avoid heavy libraries to keep emulator FAST - Target SDK: 33 (Android 13) - Min SDK: 21 - Compile SDK: 33 - Must run smoothly on low-end emulator (1GB RAM) PERFORMANCE RULES: - Do NOT block main thread - Use background threads (Coroutines or WorkManager) - Use lightweight UI (ConstraintLayout / LinearLayout only) - Avoid complex animations - Optimize for low RAM devices FEATURES: 1. Add Medicine - Medicine Name - Time (TimePicker) - Repeat (Daily) - Save button 2. View Medicine List - RecyclerView - Show name + time 3. Reminder Notification - Use AlarmManager or WorkManager - Show notification at correct time 4. Delete Medicine 5. Simple UI (no lag) STRUCTURE: Give code step by step in this order: 1. Project setup (Gradle files) 2. AndroidManifest.xml 3. Layout XML files 4. Data Model class 5. Database (Room - simple) 6. Repository 7. ViewModel 8. Adapter (RecyclerView) 9. Activities (Main + Add Medicine) 10. Alarm/Notification logic 11. Permissions IMPORTANT: - Explain WHERE to paste each file - Mention folder path (example: app/src/main/java/...) - Give FULL code (not partial) - Avoid errors - Keep code SIMPLE BONUS: - Add Login & Register using SharedPreferences (optional) - Add toggle to enable/disable reminder Make sure the app builds WITHOUT ERRORS in Android Studio. step by step process i need,layout also put for xml

Created a file, ran a command

All 30 files confirmed. Now copy to outputs:

Ran a command, read a file

All 30 files are complete and ready. Here's a full summary of everything delivered:

✅ Complete Medicine Reminder App — What You Got

📦 30 Files Across 3 Layers

LayerFiles
Gradle & Configbuild.gradle (project + app), settings.gradle, gradle.properties
ManifestAndroidManifest.xml (permissions + all activity/receiver declarations)
Data LayerMedicine.kt, MedicineDao.kt, MedicineDatabase.kt, MedicineRepository.kt
UI LayerLoginActivity, RegisterActivity, MainActivity, AddMedicineActivity, MedicineAdapter
ViewModelMedicineViewModel.kt
Alarm/NotificationAlarmScheduler.kt, NotificationHelper.kt, AlarmReceiver.kt, BootReceiver.kt
AuthSharedPrefsHelper.kt (Register + Login + Session)
Layouts5 XML files (Login, Register, Main, AddMedicine, RecyclerView item)
Resourcescolors.xml, strings.xml, themes.xml, ic_notification.xml

🔑 Key Anti-ANR Decisions Made

  • Dispatchers.IO — every Room DB operation runs on background thread
  • LiveData — DB → UI updates are automatic, no polling
  • DiffUtil in adapter — only changed RecyclerView rows redraw
  • AlarmManager instead of WorkManager — zero battery/RAM overhead for exact-time reminders
  • viewModelScope — coroutines auto-cancel on screen destroy
  • No animations, no heavy bitmaps — safe for 1GB RAM emulators

📖 Start with SETUP_GUIDE.md — it has the exact paste path for every single file and a common errors table.

mention layout names pls

Mar 29

Here are all 5 layout XML files with their exact names and paths:

#File NamePathUsed By
1activity_login.xmlapp/src/main/res/layout/activity_login.xmlLoginActivity.kt
2activity_register.xmlapp/src/main/res/layout/activity_register.xmlRegisterActivity.kt
3activity_main.xmlapp/src/main/res/layout/activity_main.xmlMainActivity.kt
4activity_add_medicine.xmlapp/src/main/res/layout/activity_add_medicine.xmlAddMedicineActivity.kt
5item_medicine.xmlapp/src/main/res/layout/item_medicine.xmlMedicineAdapter.kt (RecyclerView row)

What each layout contains:

activity_login.xml — Username + Password fields, Login button, "Go to Register" text link

activity_register.xml — Username + Password + Confirm Password fields, Register button, "Go to Login" text link

activity_main.xml — Top header bar with logout button, RecyclerView list, empty-state TextView, FAB (+) button

activity_add_medicine.xml — Medicine name input field, TimePicker (spinner style), Repeat = Daily info row, Save button

item_medicine.xml — Pill emoji icon, medicine name, time (HH:MM), enable/disable Switch toggle, delete (🗑) button — all inside a CardView

menntion layout types linear,constraint

Mar 29

Here are all 5 layouts with their root layout types:

#File NameRoot Layout TypeWhy This Was Chosen
1activity_login.xmlLinearLayout (vertical)Simple top-to-bottom form, no complex positioning needed
2activity_register.xmlLinearLayout (vertical)Same — stacked fields, straightforward
3activity_main.xmlConstraintLayoutHeader + RecyclerView + FAB need precise anchoring
4activity_add_medicine.xmlScrollView → LinearLayout (vertical)Needs scroll on small screens (TimePicker is tall)
5item_medicine.xmlCardView → ConstraintLayoutIcon + name + time + switch + delete all need 2D positioning

Inside layout breakdown:

activity_login.xml

LinearLayout (vertical) ← root
  └── TextInputLayout (username)
  └── TextInputLayout (password)
  └── MaterialButton (login)
  └── TextView (go to register)

activity_register.xml

LinearLayout (vertical) ← root
  └── TextInputLayout (username)
  └── TextInputLayout (password)
  └── TextInputLayout (confirm password)
  └── MaterialButton (register)
  └── TextView (go to login)

activity_main.xml

ConstraintLayout ← root
  └── LinearLayout (header bar)
        └── TextView (title)
        └── MaterialButton (logout)
  └── TextView (empty state message)
  └── RecyclerView (medicine list)
  └── FloatingActionButton (+ button)

activity_add_medicine.xml

ScrollView ← root
  └── LinearLayout (vertical)
        └── TextInputLayout (medicine name)
        └── CardView
              └── TimePicker (spinner)
        └── LinearLayout (repeat = daily row)
        └── MaterialButton (save)

item_medicine.xml

CardView ← root
  └── ConstraintLayout
        └── TextView (💊 icon)
        └── TextView (medicine name)
        └── TextView (time HH:MM)
        └── SwitchMaterial (enable/disable)
        └── MaterialButton (🗑 delete)

Quick rule used throughout:

  • LinearLayout → when items just stack vertically one after another
  • ConstraintLayout → when items need to be anchored relative to each other in 2D
  • ScrollView → wraps LinearLayout when content might be taller than the screen
  • CardView → wraps ConstraintLayout in list items to get shadow + rounded corners
com.medicinereminder.data.model`
   - `com.medicinereminder.data.db`
   - `com.medicinereminder.data.repository`
   - `com.medicinereminder.ui.activities`
   - `com.medicinereminder.ui.adapters`
   - `com.medicinereminder.ui.viewmodel`
   - `com.medicinereminder.receiver`
   - `com.medicinereminder.utils`
my package name is com.example.medicinereminder
Mar 29

You need to do a find & replace across all files. Here's exactly what to change:

🔁 Find & Replace

FindReplace With
com.medicineremindercom.example.medicinereminder

In Android Studio — easiest way:

  1. Press Ctrl + Shift + R (Windows/Linux) or Cmd + Shift + R (Mac)
  2. In the "Find" box type: com.medicinereminder
  3. In the "Replace" box type: com.example.medicinereminder
  4. Click "Replace All"

Also update these 2 files manually:

app/build.gradle — find this line:

gradle
applicationId "com.medicinereminder"

Change to:

gradle
applicationId "com.example.medicinereminder"

AndroidManifest.xml — find this line:

xml
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android">

Add the package attribute (if not auto-added):

xml
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    package="com.example.medicinereminder">
```

---

### Your corrected package list:
```
com.example.medicinereminder.data.model
com.example.medicinereminder.data.db
com.example.medicinereminder.data.repository
com.example.medicinereminder.ui.activities
com.example.medicinereminder.ui.adapters
com.example.medicinereminder.ui.viewmodel
com.example.medicinereminder.receiver
com.example.medicinereminder.utils

✅ After replacing:

  1. Click Build → Clean Project
  2. Click Build → Rebuild Project
  3. Run the app

That one Ctrl+Shift+R replace handles all 18 Kotlin files at once — every package declaration and every import statement gets updated automatically.

Mar 29

This error means your app/build.gradle file is being treated as Kotlin DSL (.kts) but the code is written in Groovy DSL. This happens when Android Studio creates the project with build.gradle.kts by default.

✅ Fix — Two Options:

Option A: Rename the file (Easiest)

Check if your file is named build.gradle.kts instead of build.gradle.

In Android Studio's project panel:

  1. Find app/build.gradle.kts
  2. Right-click → Refactor → Rename → remove the .kts → save as build.gradle
  3. Do the same for the project-level build.gradle.kts
  4. Click Sync Now

Option B: Rewrite for Kotlin DSL (if you want to keep .kts)

Replace your entire app/build.gradle.kts with this:

kotlin
plugins {
    id("com.android.application")
    id("org.jetbrains.kotlin.android")
    id("kotlin-kapt")
}

android {
    namespace = "com.example.medicinereminder"
    compileSdk = 33

    defaultConfig {
        applicationId = "com.example.medicinereminder"
        minSdk = 21
        targetSdk = 33
        versionCode = 1
        versionName = "1.0"
    }

    buildTypes {
        release {
            isMinifyEnabled = false
            proguardFiles(
                getDefaultProguardFile("proguard-android-optimize.txt"),
                "proguard-rules.pro"
            )
        }
    }

    compileOptions {
        sourceCompatibility = JavaVersion.VERSION_1_8
        targetCompatibility = JavaVersion.VERSION_1_8
    }

    kotlinOptions {
        jvmTarget = "1.8"
    }

    buildFeatures {
        viewBinding = true
    }
}

dependencies {
    implementation("androidx.core:core-ktx:1.10.1")
    implementation("androidx.appcompat:appcompat:1.6.1")
    implementation("com.google.android.material:material:1.9.0")
    implementation("androidx.constraintlayout:constraintlayout:2.1.4")

    implementation("androidx.lifecycle:lifecycle-viewmodel-ktx:2.6.1")
    implementation("androidx.lifecycle:lifecycle-livedata-ktx:2.6.1")
    implementation("androidx.activity:activity-ktx:1.7.2")

    implementation("androidx.room:room-runtime:2.5.2")
    implementation("androidx.room:room-ktx:2.5.2")
    kapt("androidx.room:room-compiler:2.5.2")

    implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-android:1.7.1")
    implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-core:1.7.1")

    implementation("androidx.recyclerview:recyclerview:1.3.1")
}

And replace your project-level build.gradle.kts with this:

kotlin
// Project-level build.gradle.kts
plugins {
    id("com.android.application") version "8.0.2" apply false
    id("org.jetbrains.kotlin.android") version "1.8.0" apply false
}

After either fix:

  1. File → Sync Project with Gradle Files
  2. Build → Clean Project
  3. Build → Rebuild Project

Quick way to tell which DSL you have: open the file — if it uses = signs like compileSdk = 33 it's Kotlin DSL (.kts). If it uses spaces like compileSdk 33 it's Groovy (.gradle). Use Option B code above for .kts.

nable to find method ''org.gradle.api.artifacts.Dependency org.gradle.api.artifacts.dsl.DependencyHandler.module(java.lang.Object)'' 'org.gradle.api.artifacts.Dependency org.gradle.api.artifacts.dsl.DependencyHandler.module(java.lang.Object)' Gradle's dependency cache may be corrupt (this sometimes occurs after a network connection timeout.) Re-download dependencies and sync project (requires network) The state of a Gradle build process (daemon) may be corrupt. Stopping all Gradle daemons may solve this problem. Stop Gradle build processes (requires restart) Your project may be using a third-party plugin which is not compatible with the other plugins in the project or the version of Gradle requested by the project. In the case of corrupt Gradle processes, you can also try closing the IDE and then killing all Java processes.

Mar 29

This is a Gradle version compatibility issue. Fix it step by step:

Step 1 — Stop Gradle Daemons

In Android Studio:

  1. Click File → Invalidate Caches → Invalidate and Restart
  2. After restart, go to View → Tool Windows → Terminal
  3. Type this command:
./gradlew --stop

Step 2 — Fix gradle-wrapper.properties

Find this file: Path: gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties

Replace the entire content with:

properties
distributionBase=GRADLE_USER_HOME
distributionPath=wrapper/dists
zipStoreBase=GRADLE_USER_HOME
zipStorePath=wrapper/dists
distributionUrl=https\://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-8.0-bin.zip

Step 3 — Fix Project-level build.gradle.kts

Path: build.gradle.kts (project level — NOT app level)

Replace entirely with:

kotlin
plugins {
    id("com.android.application") version "8.0.2" apply false
    id("org.jetbrains.kotlin.android") version "1.8.20" apply false
}

Step 4 — Fix settings.gradle.kts

Path: settings.gradle.kts

Replace entirely with:

kotlin
pluginManagement {
    repositories {
        google()
        mavenCentral()
        gradlePluginPortal()
    }
}
dependencyResolutionManagement {
    repositoriesMode.set(RepositoriesMode.FAIL_ON_PROJECT_REPOS)
    repositories {
        google()
        mavenCentral()
    }
}
rootProject.name = "MedicineReminder"
include(":app")
```

---

## Step 5 — Clean everything manually

In Terminal inside Android Studio:

**Windows:**
```
cd android  
rmdir /s /q .gradle
rmdir /s /q build
rmdir /s /q app\build

Mac/Linux:

bash
rm -rf .gradle build app/build

Step 6 — Sync & Rebuild

  1. Click File → Sync Project with Gradle Files
  2. Wait for it to finish downloading
  3. Click Build → Clean Project
  4. Click Build → Rebuild Project

✅ Version Compatibility Table (must match exactly)

ToolVersion
Gradle8.0
Android Gradle Plugin8.0.2
Kotlin1.8.20
Compile SDK33
Target SDK33

If it still fails — check Java version

Go to File → Settings → Build, Execution, Deployment → Build Tools → Gradle

Set Gradle JDK to → JDK 17 (not 11, not 19)

If JDK 17 is not listed → click Download JDK → select version 17 → vendor Eclipse Temurin → click Download.

This is the most common hidden cause of this exact error.

what i have do for package name you give diffrent i have com.example.medicinereminder.

Mar 29

Your package name com.example.medicinereminder is correct. Here's exactly what to check in 3 places:

Place 1 — app/build.gradle.kts

Make sure these 2 lines match:

kotlin
namespace = "com.example.medicinereminder"
applicationId = "com.example.medicinereminder"

Place 2 — Every Kotlin file top line

Every .kt file must start with your package. For example:

Medicine.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.data.model

MedicineDao.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.data.db

MedicineDatabase.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.data.db

MedicineRepository.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.data.repository

MedicineViewModel.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.ui.viewmodel

MedicineAdapter.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.ui.adapters

LoginActivity.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.ui.activities

RegisterActivity.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.ui.activities

MainActivity.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.ui.activities

AddMedicineActivity.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.ui.activities

AlarmReceiver.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

BootReceiver.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

AlarmScheduler.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.utils

NotificationHelper.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.utils

SharedPrefsHelper.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.utils

Place 3 — AndroidManifest.xml

All activity and receiver names must use full path:

xml
<activity android:name=".ui.activities.LoginActivity" ... />
<activity android:name=".ui.activities.RegisterActivity" ... />
<activity android:name=".ui.activities.MainActivity" ... />
<activity android:name=".ui.activities.AddMedicineActivity" ... />
<receiver android:name=".receiver.AlarmReceiver" ... />
<receiver android:name=".receiver.BootReceiver" ... />

The dot . at the start means Android auto-prepends com.example.medicinereminder — so these are fine as-is.

Quickest fix in Android Studio

Press Ctrl+Shift+R (Windows) or Cmd+Shift+R (Mac):

  • Find: com.medicinereminder
  • Replace: com.example.medicinereminder
  • Click Replace All

Then:

  1. Build → Clean Project
  2. Build → Rebuild Project

That's it — one replace fixes all 15 files at once. ✅

Incompatible Gradle JVM version The project's Gradle version 8.0 is incompatible with the Gradle JVM version 21 currently selected to run Gradle build. Gradle 8.0 supports Java versions between 1.8 and 19. Please update the selected JVM to a compatible version. Apply compatible Gradle JDK configuration and sync Change Gradle JDK configuration

Mar 29

Easy fix! You just need to change the JDK version. Follow these steps:

Fix — Change Gradle JDK to 17

File → Settings (Windows) or Android Studio → Preferences (Mac)

Then navigate:

Build, Execution, Deployment
    → Build Tools
        → Gradle

Find "Gradle JDK" dropdown → change from 21 to 17

If JDK 17 is not in the list — Download it

In the same Gradle JDK dropdown:

  1. Click "Download JDK..."
  2. Set:
    • Version: 17
    • Vendor: Eclipse Temurin (or Amazon Corretto)
  3. Click Download
  4. Wait for download to finish
  5. Select it from the dropdown
  6. Click OK

OR — Upgrade Gradle to support Java 21

If you don't want to change JDK, update Gradle version instead.

Path: gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties

Change this line:

properties
# Change FROM this:
distributionUrl=https\://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-8.0-bin.zip

# Change TO this:
distributionUrl=https\://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-8.9-bin.zip

And update build.gradle.kts (project level):

kotlin
plugins {
    id("com.android.application") version "8.3.2" apply false
    id("org.jetbrains.kotlin.android") version "1.9.0" apply false
}

✅ Java & Gradle Compatibility Table

Java / JDK VersionGradle Version
JDK 17Gradle 8.0 ✅
JDK 21Gradle 8.9+ ✅
JDK 11Gradle 7.x ✅

Recommended Choice

Option 1 (easiest): Just click "Apply compatible Gradle JDK configuration and sync" — the blue link shown in your error message. Android Studio will auto-fix it for you.

Then:

  1. File → Sync Project with Gradle Files
  2. Build → Clean Project
  3. Build → Rebuild Project
Mar 29

The error points to this file:

com/example/medicinereminder/ui/theme/Theme.kt

This is a Jetpack Compose file that Android Studio auto-generated when you created the project. It's conflicting with our XML-based project.

Fix — Delete 3 Auto-Generated Compose Files

In Android Studio's project panel, navigate to:

app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/ui/theme/

You will see these 3 files — delete all of them:

  • Theme.kt ← main culprit
  • Color.kt
  • Type.kt

Right-click each → Delete → click OK

Also remove the theme folder itself

After deleting the 3 files, right-click the empty theme folder → Delete

Also check app/build.gradle.kts — remove Compose dependencies

Open app/build.gradle.kts and make sure these lines are NOT present. If you see any of these, delete them:

kotlin
// DELETE these if they exist:
implementation("androidx.compose.ui:ui:...")
implementation("androidx.compose.material3:material3:...")
implementation("androidx.activity:activity-compose:...")
implementation("androidx.compose.ui:ui-tooling-preview:...")

// Also remove this from android {} block if present:
buildFeatures {
    compose = true   // ← DELETE this line
}
composeOptions {
    kotlinCompilerExtensionVersion = "..."  // ← DELETE this block
}

Your buildFeatures block should only have:

kotlin
buildFeatures {
    viewBinding = true
}

After deleting:

  1. Build → Clean Project
  2. Build → Rebuild Project

Why this happened

When you create a new project in Android Studio and select "Empty Activity", newer versions of Android Studio automatically add Compose files and dependencies. Since our app uses XML layouts only, those Compose files must be removed. They are completely unnecessary for this project.

  • failed
  • Download info
  • :app:kaptDebugKotlin
  • MedicineDao.java
  • Not sure how to convert a Cursor to this method's return type (java.lang.Object).
  • Query method parameters should either be a type that can be converted into a database column or a List / Array that contains such type. You can consider adding a Type Adapter for this.
  • Unused parameter: $completion
  • Type of the parameter must be a class annotated with @Entity or a collection/array of it.
  • Not sure how to handle insert method's return type.
  • Not sure how to handle delete method's return type. Currently the supported return types are void, int or Int.
  • Type of the parameter must be a class annotated with @Entity or a collection/array of it.
  • Type of the parameter must be a class annotated with @Entity or a collection/array of it.
  • Not sure how to handle update method's return type. Currently the supported return types are void, int or Int.
  • The query returns some columns [id, name, hour, minute, isEnabled] which are not used by java.lang.Object. You can use @ColumnInfo annotation on the fields to specify the mapping. You can annotate the method with @RewriteQueriesToDropUnusedColumns to direct Room to rewrite your query to avoid fetching unused columns. You can suppress this warning by annotating the method with @SuppressWarnings(RoomWarnings.CURSOR_MISMATCH). Columns returned by the query: id, name, hour, minute, isEnabled.
  • java.lang.reflect.InvocationTargetException (no error message)
Mar 30

This error is a Room + Kotlin Coroutines version mismatch. The fix is to replace your entire MedicineDao.kt with a version that Room can process correctly.

Fix — Replace MedicineDao.kt completely

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/data/db/MedicineDao.kt

Replace the entire file with this:

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.data.db

import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
import androidx.room.*
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.model.Medicine

@Dao
interface MedicineDao {

    @Query("SELECT * FROM medicines ORDER BY hour ASC, minute ASC")
    fun getAllMedicines(): LiveData<List<Medicine>>

    @Query("SELECT * FROM medicines WHERE isEnabled = 1")
    fun getEnabledMedicinesSync(): List<Medicine>

    @Insert(onConflict = OnConflictStrategy.REPLACE)
    fun insert(medicine: Medicine): Long

    @Delete
    fun delete(medicine: Medicine)

    @Update
    fun update(medicine: Medicine)
}

Also update MedicineRepository.kt

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/data/repository/MedicineRepository.kt

Replace entirely with:

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.data.repository

import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.db.MedicineDao
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.model.Medicine

class MedicineRepository(private val dao: MedicineDao) {

    val allMedicines: LiveData<List<Medicine>> = dao.getAllMedicines()

    fun insert(medicine: Medicine): Long {
        return dao.insert(medicine)
    }

    fun delete(medicine: Medicine) {
        dao.delete(medicine)
    }

    fun update(medicine: Medicine) {
        dao.update(medicine)
    }

    fun getEnabledMedicines(): List<Medicine> {
        return dao.getEnabledMedicinesSync()
    }
}

Also update MedicineViewModel.kt

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/ui/viewmodel/MedicineViewModel.kt

Replace entirely with:

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.ui.viewmodel

import android.app.Application
import androidx.lifecycle.AndroidViewModel
import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.db.MedicineDatabase
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.model.Medicine
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.repository.MedicineRepository
import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
import kotlinx.coroutines.launch

class MedicineViewModel(application: Application) : AndroidViewModel(application) {

    private val repository: MedicineRepository
    val allMedicines: LiveData<List<Medicine>>

    init {
        val dao = MedicineDatabase.getDatabase(application).medicineDao()
        repository = MedicineRepository(dao)
        allMedicines = repository.allMedicines
    }

    fun insert(medicine: Medicine, onInserted: (Long) -> Unit) {
        viewModelScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
            val id = repository.insert(medicine)
            launch(Dispatchers.Main) {
                onInserted(id)
            }
        }
    }

    fun delete(medicine: Medicine) {
        viewModelScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
            repository.delete(medicine)
        }
    }

    fun update(medicine: Medicine) {
        viewModelScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
            repository.update(medicine)
        }
    }

    fun getEnabledMedicines(): List<Medicine> {
        return repository.getEnabledMedicines()
    }
}

Also update BootReceiver.kt

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/receiver/BootReceiver.kt

Replace entirely with:

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
import android.content.Context
import android.content.Intent
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.db.MedicineDatabase
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.repository.MedicineRepository
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.AlarmScheduler
import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
import kotlinx.coroutines.launch

class BootReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {

    override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
        if (intent.action != Intent.ACTION_BOOT_COMPLETED) return

        CoroutineScope(Dispatchers.IO).launch {
            val dao = MedicineDatabase.getDatabase(context).medicineDao()
            val repository = MedicineRepository(dao)
            val medicines = repository.getEnabledMedicines()
            medicines.forEach { medicine ->
                AlarmScheduler.scheduleAlarm(context, medicine)
            }
        }
    }
}

Why this happened

The old code used suspend functions in the DAO. Older versions of the Room kapt compiler don't correctly process suspend functions — it sees Continuation<?> as an unknown type and throws those errors. The fix is to use plain (non-suspend) functions in the DAO and handle threading in the ViewModel using Dispatchers.IO instead.

After replacing all 4 files:

  1. Build → Clean Project
  2. Build → Rebuild Project

  • Not sure how to convert a Cursor to this method's return type (androidx.lifecycle.LiveData>).
  • There is a problem with the query: [SQLITE_ERROR] SQL error or missing database (no such table: medicines)
  • Not sure how to convert a Cursor to this method's return type (java.util.List).
  • java.lang.reflect.InvocationTargetException (no error message)
Mar 30

The problem is Room can't find the medicines table — this means it's not seeing the @Entity annotation on Medicine.kt properly. Fix all 3 files below:

Fix 1 — Replace Medicine.kt

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/data/model/Medicine.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.data.model

import androidx.room.ColumnInfo
import androidx.room.Entity
import androidx.room.PrimaryKey

@Entity(tableName = "medicines")
data class Medicine(
    @PrimaryKey(autoGenerate = true)
    @ColumnInfo(name = "id")
    val id: Int = 0,

    @ColumnInfo(name = "name")
    val name: String,

    @ColumnInfo(name = "hour")
    val hour: Int,

    @ColumnInfo(name = "minute")
    val minute: Int,

    @ColumnInfo(name = "isEnabled")
    val isEnabled: Boolean = true
)

Fix 2 — Replace MedicineDao.kt

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/data/db/MedicineDao.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.data.db

import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
import androidx.room.Dao
import androidx.room.Delete
import androidx.room.Insert
import androidx.room.OnConflictStrategy
import androidx.room.Query
import androidx.room.Update
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.model.Medicine

@Dao
interface MedicineDao {

    @Query("SELECT * FROM medicines ORDER BY hour ASC, minute ASC")
    fun getAllMedicines(): LiveData<List<Medicine>>

    @Query("SELECT * FROM medicines WHERE isEnabled = 1")
    fun getEnabledMedicinesSync(): List<Medicine>

    @Insert(onConflict = OnConflictStrategy.REPLACE)
    fun insert(medicine: Medicine): Long

    @Delete
    fun delete(medicine: Medicine)

    @Update
    fun update(medicine: Medicine)
}

Fix 3 — Replace MedicineDatabase.kt

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/data/db/MedicineDatabase.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.data.db

import android.content.Context
import androidx.room.Database
import androidx.room.Room
import androidx.room.RoomDatabase
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.model.Medicine

@Database(
    entities = [Medicine::class],
    version = 1,
    exportSchema = false
)
abstract class MedicineDatabase : RoomDatabase() {

    abstract fun medicineDao(): MedicineDao

    companion object {
        @Volatile
        private var INSTANCE: MedicineDatabase? = null

        fun getDatabase(context: Context): MedicineDatabase {
            return INSTANCE ?: synchronized(this) {
                val instance = Room.databaseBuilder(
                    context.applicationContext,
                    MedicineDatabase::class.java,
                    "medicine_database"
                )
                .fallbackToDestructiveMigration()
                .build()
                INSTANCE = instance
                instance
            }
        }
    }
}

Fix 4 — Check app/build.gradle.kts has kapt correctly

Make sure these 3 lines are all present and correct:

kotlin
// At top in plugins block:
id("kotlin-kapt")

// In dependencies block:
implementation("androidx.room:room-runtime:2.5.2")
implementation("androidx.room:room-ktx:2.5.2")
kapt("androidx.room:room-compiler:2.5.2")
```

---

## After all fixes:

Run these in order:

**Terminal in Android Studio:**
```
./gradlew clean

Then:

  1. Build → Clean Project
  2. Build → Rebuild Project

Why this happened

Room's annotation processor (kapt) processes files in isolation. If Medicine.kt doesn't have explicit @ColumnInfo on every field, or if the import for @Entity is ambiguous, kapt sometimes fails to register the table. Adding explicit @ColumnInfo to every field and explicit imports (no wildcard *) to the DAO forces kapt to resolve everything correctly.

  • Overload resolution ambiguity: public inline fun Iterable.forEach(action: (TypeVariable(T)) -> Unit): Unit defined in kotlin.collections public inline fun Map.forEach(action: (Map.Entry) -> Unit): Unit defined in kotlin.collections
  • Cannot infer a type for this parameter. Please specify it explicitly.
  • BootReceiver.kt
  • AddMedicineActivity.kt
  • MedicineVewModel.kt

You have duplicate files and some type issues. Let me fix everything one by one.

Problem 1 — Duplicate BootReceiver.kt

You likely have two BootReceiver files. Check these locations and delete one:

app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/receiver/BootReceiver.kt  ← KEEP this
app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/BootReceiver.kt           ← DELETE this

Fix 1 — Replace BootReceiver.kt (keep only ONE)

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/receiver/BootReceiver.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
import android.content.Context
import android.content.Intent
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.db.MedicineDatabase
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.AlarmScheduler
import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
import kotlinx.coroutines.launch

class BootReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {

    override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
        if (intent.action != Intent.ACTION_BOOT_COMPLETED) return

        CoroutineScope(Dispatchers.IO).launch {
            val dao = MedicineDatabase.getDatabase(context).medicineDao()
            val medicines = dao.getEnabledMedicinesSync()
            medicines.forEach { medicine ->
                AlarmScheduler.scheduleAlarm(context, medicine)
            }
        }
    }
}

Fix 2 — Replace MedicineRepository.kt

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/data/repository/MedicineRepository.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.data.repository

import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.db.MedicineDao
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.model.Medicine

class MedicineRepository(private val dao: MedicineDao) {

    val allMedicines: LiveData<List<Medicine>> = dao.getAllMedicines()

    fun insert(medicine: Medicine): Long {
        return dao.insert(medicine)
    }

    fun delete(medicine: Medicine) {
        dao.delete(medicine)
    }

    fun update(medicine: Medicine) {
        dao.update(medicine)
    }

    fun getEnabledMedicines(): List<Medicine> {
        return dao.getEnabledMedicinesSync()
    }
}

Fix 3 — Replace MedicineViewModel.kt

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/ui/viewmodel/MedicineViewModel.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.ui.viewmodel

import android.app.Application
import androidx.lifecycle.AndroidViewModel
import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.db.MedicineDatabase
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.model.Medicine
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.repository.MedicineRepository
import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
import kotlinx.coroutines.launch

class MedicineViewModel(application: Application) : AndroidViewModel(application) {

    private val repo: MedicineRepository
    val allMedicines: LiveData<List<Medicine>>

    init {
        val dao = MedicineDatabase.getDatabase(application).medicineDao()
        repo = MedicineRepository(dao)
        allMedicines = repo.allMedicines
    }

    fun insert(medicine: Medicine, onInserted: (Long) -> Unit) {
        viewModelScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
            val id = repo.insert(medicine)
            launch(Dispatchers.Main) {
                onInserted(id)
            }
        }
    }

    fun delete(medicine: Medicine) {
        viewModelScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
            repo.delete(medicine)
        }
    }

    fun update(medicine: Medicine) {
        viewModelScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) {
            repo.update(medicine)
        }
    }
}

Fix 4 — Replace AddMedicineActivity.kt

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/ui/activities/AddMedicineActivity.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.ui.activities

import android.os.Bundle
import android.widget.Toast
import androidx.activity.viewModels
import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.model.Medicine
import com.example.medicinereminder.databinding.ActivityAddMedicineBinding
import com.example.medicinereminder.ui.viewmodel.MedicineViewModel
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.AlarmScheduler

class AddMedicineActivity : AppCompatActivity() {

    private lateinit var binding: ActivityAddMedicineBinding
    private val viewModel: MedicineViewModel by viewModels()

    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
        binding = ActivityAddMedicineBinding.inflate(layoutInflater)
        setContentView(binding.root)

        supportActionBar?.title = "Add Medicine"
        supportActionBar?.setDisplayHomeAsUpEnabled(true)

        binding.timePicker.setIs24HourView(true)

        binding.btnSave.setOnClickListener {
            saveMedicine()
        }
    }

    private fun saveMedicine() {
        val name = binding.etMedicineName.text.toString().trim()

        if (name.isEmpty()) {
            binding.etMedicineName.error = "Please enter medicine name"
            binding.etMedicineName.requestFocus()
            return
        }

        val hour = binding.timePicker.hour
        val minute = binding.timePicker.minute

        val medicine = Medicine(
            name = name,
            hour = hour,
            minute = minute,
            isEnabled = true
        )

        viewModel.insert(medicine) { newId: Long ->
            val saved = medicine.copy(id = newId.toInt())
            AlarmScheduler.scheduleAlarm(this@AddMedicineActivity, saved)
            Toast.makeText(
                this@AddMedicineActivity,
                "Reminder set for $name at ${String.format("%02d:%02d", hour, minute)}",
                Toast.LENGTH_LONG
            ).show()
            finish()
        }
    }

    override fun onSupportNavigateUp(): Boolean {
        onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
        return true
    }
}

This error means your BootReceiver.kt file has extra code or characters at the top before the package declaration. Let me give you a clean file.

Action 1 — DELETE the existing file completely

In Android Studio:

  • Find BootReceiver.kt in receiver folder
  • Right-click → Delete → OK

Action 2 — Create a brand NEW file

Right-click on receiver folder → New → Kotlin Class/File → name it BootReceiver

Then paste exactly this — no extra spaces, no extra lines at top:

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
import android.content.Context
import android.content.Intent
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.db.MedicineDatabase
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.AlarmScheduler
import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
import kotlinx.coroutines.launch

class BootReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {

    override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
        if (intent.action != Intent.ACTION_BOOT_COMPLETED) return

        CoroutineScope(Dispatchers.IO).launch {
            val dao = MedicineDatabase.getDatabase(context).medicineDao()
            val medicines = dao.getEnabledMedicinesSync()
            medicines.forEach { medicine ->
                AlarmScheduler.scheduleAlarm(context, medicine)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

---

## Why this error happens

The error `"imports are only allowed at beginning of file"` means one of these things happened in your file:
```
// BAD - some random text before package
Here is the BootReceiver code:    ← this breaks it

package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver
...
```
```
// BAD - two classes merged into one file
class BootReceiver { ... }

package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver   ← package in wrong place
class SomethingElse { ... }
```
```
// GOOD - package must be the very first line
package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

import ...

class BootReceiver { ... }

Also do this for ALL your Kotlin files

Open each file and make sure line 1 is always the package declaration with nothing above it:

FileLine 1 must be
BootReceiver.ktpackage com.example.medicinereminder.receiver
AlarmReceiver.ktpackage com.example.medicinereminder.receiver
Medicine.ktpackage com.example.medicinereminder.data.model
MedicineDao.ktpackage com.example.medicinereminder.data.db
MedicineDatabase.ktpackage com.example.medicinereminder.data.db
MedicineRepository.ktpackage com.example.medicinereminder.data.repository
MedicineViewModel.ktpackage com.example.medicinereminder.ui.viewmodel
MedicineAdapter.ktpackage com.example.medicinereminder.ui.adapters
AlarmScheduler.ktpackage com.example.medicinereminder.utils
NotificationHelper.ktpackage com.example.medicinereminder.utils
SharedPrefsHelper.ktpackage com.example.medicinereminder.utils

After fixing:

  1. Build → Clean Project
  2. Build → Rebuild Project

You have two BootReceiver classes somewhere in your project. Let's find and fix them.

Step 1 — Find ALL BootReceiver files

In Android Studio press Ctrl + Shift + F (Find in Files)

Search for:

class BootReceiver

It will show you every file that contains this. You should see it in 2 places — you need to delete one.

Step 2 — Check these exact locations

Look in your project panel for BootReceiver.kt in ANY of these folders and delete all except one:

app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/receiver/BootReceiver.kt  ← KEEP
app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/BootReceiver.kt            ← DELETE
app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/ui/BootReceiver.kt         ← DELETE
app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/ui/activities/BootReceiver.kt ← DELETE

Step 3 — Also check if two classes are in ONE file

Open every .kt file in the receiver folder. Sometimes two classes get pasted into one file by accident.

For example AlarmReceiver.kt might accidentally contain:

kotlin
// AlarmReceiver.kt — BAD example
class AlarmReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {
    ...
}

// accidentally pasted here too:
class BootReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {   ← DELETE THIS from AlarmReceiver.kt
    ...
}
```

---

## Step 4 — The `receiver` folder should have EXACTLY 2 files
```
receiver/
  ├── AlarmReceiver.kt    ← only AlarmReceiver class inside
  └── BootReceiver.kt     ← only BootReceiver class inside
```

---

## Step 5 — After deleting the duplicate

1. **Build → Clean Project**
2. **Build → Rebuild Project**

---

## Quick way to see all files

In Android Studio, switch the left panel from **Android view** to **Project view**:

Click the dropdown at top of project panel that says **"Android"** → change to **"Project"**

Then navigate to:
```
MedicineReminder → app → src → main → java → com → example → medicinereminder

You will see every single folder and file — find and delete any duplicate BootReceiver.kt.

package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
import android.content.Context
import android.content.Intent
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.NotificationHelper

/**
 * AlarmReceiver: triggered by AlarmManager at the scheduled time.
 * Runs on main thread but VERY briefly (just shows notification).
 * Do NOT do heavy work here.
 */
class AlarmReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {

    override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
        val medicineId = intent.getIntExtra("medicine_id", -1)
        val medicineName = intent.getStringExtra("medicine_name") ?: "Medicine"

        if (medicineId == -1) return

        // Create notification channel (safe to call every time)
        NotificationHelper.createNotificationChannel(context)

        // Show the notification
        NotificationHelper.showNotification(context, medicineId, medicineName)
    }
}
Mar 30

Your AlarmReceiver.kt looks perfectly fine! The issue is that BootReceiver class is accidentally pasted inside AlarmReceiver.kt at the bottom.

Check the BOTTOM of your AlarmReceiver.kt

Scroll to the very end of that file. It probably looks like this:

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
import android.content.Context
import android.content.Intent
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.NotificationHelper

class AlarmReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {

    override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
        val medicineId = intent.getIntExtra("medicine_id", -1)
        val medicineName = intent.getStringExtra("medicine_name") ?: "Medicine"

        if (medicineId == -1) return

        NotificationHelper.createNotificationChannel(context)
        NotificationHelper.showNotification(context, medicineId, medicineName)
    }
}

// ↓↓↓ DELETE EVERYTHING BELOW THIS LINE ↓↓↓
package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
...
class BootReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {   ← THIS is causing the error
    ...
}

Fix — Make AlarmReceiver.kt contain ONLY this:

Delete everything and replace with exactly this:

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
import android.content.Context
import android.content.Intent
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.NotificationHelper

class AlarmReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {

    override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
        val medicineId = intent.getIntExtra("medicine_id", -1)
        val medicineName = intent.getStringExtra("medicine_name") ?: "Medicine"

        if (medicineId == -1) return

        NotificationHelper.createNotificationChannel(context)
        NotificationHelper.showNotification(context, medicineId, medicineName)
    }
}

Then verify BootReceiver.kt has ONLY this:

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
import android.content.Context
import android.content.Intent
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.db.MedicineDatabase
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.AlarmScheduler
import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
import kotlinx.coroutines.launch

class BootReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {

    override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
        if (intent.action != Intent.ACTION_BOOT_COMPLETED) return

        CoroutineScope(Dispatchers.IO).launch {
            val dao = MedicineDatabase.getDatabase(context).medicineDao()
            val medicines = dao.getEnabledMedicinesSync()
            medicines.forEach { medicine ->
                AlarmScheduler.scheduleAlarm(context, medicine)
            }
        }
    }
}

Rule to remember

In Kotlin — one class per file, always. Never paste two classes into the same .kt file unless they are intentionally nested inside each other.

Then: Build → Clean Project → Rebuild Project ✅

package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
import android.content.Context
import android.content.Intent
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.db.MedicineDatabase
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.repository.MedicineRepository
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.AlarmScheduler
import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
import kotlinx.coroutines.launch

class BootReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {

    override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
        if (intent.action != Intent.ACTION_BOOT_COMPLETED) return

        CoroutineScope(Dispatchers.IO).launch {
            val dao = MedicineDatabase.getDatabase(context).medicineDao()
            val repository = MedicineRepository(dao)
            val medicines = repository.getEnabledMedicines()
            medicines.forEach { medicine ->
                AlarmScheduler.scheduleAlarm(context, medicine)
            }
        }
    }
}
Mar 30

Your BootReceiver.kt has an old version that uses MedicineRepository — but we removed that dependency. Replace it with the simpler direct DAO version.

Replace BootReceiver.kt with exactly this:

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.receiver

import android.content.BroadcastReceiver
import android.content.Context
import android.content.Intent
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.db.MedicineDatabase
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.AlarmScheduler
import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
import kotlinx.coroutines.launch

class BootReceiver : BroadcastReceiver() {

    override fun onReceive(context: Context, intent: Intent) {
        if (intent.action != Intent.ACTION_BOOT_COMPLETED) return

        CoroutineScope(Dispatchers.IO).launch {
            val dao = MedicineDatabase.getDatabase(context).medicineDao()
            val medicines = dao.getEnabledMedicinesSync()
            medicines.forEach { medicine ->
                AlarmScheduler.scheduleAlarm(context, medicine)
            }
        }
    }
}

What changed — 3 lines removed:

RemovedReason
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.repository.MedicineRepositoryNo longer needed
val repository = MedicineRepository(dao)Skipping repository layer
repository.getEnabledMedicines()Replaced with dao.getEnabledMedicinesSync() directly

The DAO already has getEnabledMedicinesSync() — no need to go through repository in this case.

After replacing → Build → Clean Project → Rebuild Project ✅

medicine reppository code

Mar 30

Here is the complete MedicineRepository.kt:

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/data/repository/MedicineRepository.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.data.repository

import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.db.MedicineDao
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.model.Medicine

class MedicineRepository(private val dao: MedicineDao) {

    val allMedicines: LiveData<List<Medicine>> = dao.getAllMedicines()

    fun insert(medicine: Medicine): Long {
        return dao.insert(medicine)
    }

    fun delete(medicine: Medicine) {
        dao.delete(medicine)
    }

    fun update(medicine: Medicine) {
        dao.update(medicine)
    }

    fun getEnabledMedicines(): List<Medicine> {
        return dao.getEnabledMedicinesSync()
    }
}

What each function does:

FunctionPurpose
allMedicinesLiveData list — UI observes this, auto-updates when DB changes
insert()Saves new medicine to DB, returns new row ID
delete()Removes medicine from DB
update()Updates existing medicine (used for enable/disable toggle)
getEnabledMedicines()Returns list of enabled medicines — used in BootReceiver after reboot

After pasting → Build → Clean Project → Rebuild Project ✅

Function "getEnabledMedicines" is never used

Mar 30

That warning appears because BootReceiver.kt now calls dao.getEnabledMedicinesSync() directly instead of going through the repository.

You have two options:

Option A — Just ignore the warning (easiest)

It is only a warning, not an error. The app will still build and run fine. You can leave it as is.

Option B — Suppress the warning (clean code)

Add @Suppress annotation above the function in MedicineRepository.kt:

kotlin
@Suppress("unused")
fun getEnabledMedicines(): List<Medicine> {
    return dao.getEnabledMedicinesSync()
}

Option C — Delete the unused function (cleanest)

Since BootReceiver no longer uses it, just remove it from MedicineRepository.kt:

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.data.repository

import androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.db.MedicineDao
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.model.Medicine

class MedicineRepository(private val dao: MedicineDao) {

    val allMedicines: LiveData<List<Medicine>> = dao.getAllMedicines()

    fun insert(medicine: Medicine): Long {
        return dao.insert(medicine)
    }

    fun delete(medicine: Medicine) {
        dao.delete(medicine)
    }

    fun update(medicine: Medicine) {
        dao.update(medicine)
    }
}

Recommendation → go with Option C (delete it). It keeps the code clean with no unused functions and no warnings. Then Build → Rebuild Project ✅

The application could not be installed. Installation failed due to: 'Error code: 'UNKNOWN', message='Unknown failure: 'cmd: Can't find service: package''' List of apks: [0] 'C:\Users\acer\AndroidStudioProjects\MedicineReminder\app\build\intermediates\apk\debug\app-debug.apk'

Mar 30

This is an emulator issue, not a code issue. Your app compiled successfully! 🎉 The APK was built — it just can't install on the emulator.

Fix 1 — Restart the Emulator (try first)

  1. In Android Studio click Device Manager (right side panel)
  2. Click the Stop button on your emulator
  3. Wait 10 seconds
  4. Click Play to start it again
  5. Wait for emulator to fully boot (see home screen)
  6. Run the app again

Fix 2 — Cold Boot the Emulator

  1. Open Device Manager
  2. Click the 3 dots (⋮) next to your emulator
  3. Click Cold Boot Now
  4. Wait for full boot
  5. Run app again

Fix 3 — Wipe Emulator Data

  1. Open Device Manager
  2. Click 3 dots (⋮) next to emulator
  3. Click Wipe Data
  4. Click Yes
  5. Start emulator and run app again

Fix 4 — Create a New Emulator

Your current emulator may be corrupted. Create a fresh one:

  1. Open Device Manager
  2. Click Create Device
  3. Select:
    • Hardware: Pixel 4
    • Click Next
  4. Select system image:
    • API Level: 30 (Android 11) ← more stable than API 33 for emulators
    • Click Download if needed
    • Click Next
  5. Click Finish
  6. Start the new emulator
  7. Run your app

Fix 5 — Run on Real Device (most reliable)

If emulator keeps failing:

  1. On your Android phone go to Settings → About Phone
  2. Tap Build Number 7 times → Developer Mode enabled
  3. Go to Settings → Developer Options
  4. Enable USB Debugging
  5. Connect phone to PC with USB cable
  6. Allow USB debugging when prompted on phone
  7. In Android Studio select your phone from device dropdown
  8. Click Run

Quick Check — Is emulator fully booted?

The emulator must show the home screen before installing. If it shows a black screen or boot animation, wait longer.

Emulator StateCan Install?
Black screen❌ No — wait
Boot animation❌ No — wait
Lock screen✅ Yes
Home screen✅ Yes

Most likely Fix 1 or Fix 2 will solve it — this is a very common emulator crash that a simple restart fixes. ✅

The application could not be installed: INSTALL_FAILED_VERIFICATION_FAILURE Installation failed due to: 'Error code: 'INSTALL_FAILED_VERIFICATION_FAILURE', message='INSTALL_FAILED_VERIFICATION_FAILURE: Install not allowed for file:///data/app/vmdl69792394.tmp'' List of apks: [0] 'C:\Users\acer\AndroidStudioProjects\MedicineReminder\app\build\intermediates\apk\debug\app-debug.apk'

Mar 30

This is an emulator security setting blocking installation. Easy fix!

Fix 1 — Disable Play Protect on Emulator (most common fix)

On the emulator:

  1. Open Google Play Store app
  2. Tap your profile icon (top right)
  3. Tap Play Protect
  4. Tap the Settings gear icon
  5. Turn OFF "Scan apps with Play Protect"
  6. Run app again from Android Studio

Fix 2 — Disable Verification via ADB (fastest)

In Android Studio go to View → Tool Windows → Terminal

Type these commands one by one:

bash
adb shell settings put global verifier_verify_adb_installs 0
bash
adb shell settings put global package_verifier_enable 0

Then run the app again.

Fix 3 — Use an Emulator WITHOUT Google Play

The INSTALL_FAILED_VERIFICATION_FAILURE error mostly happens on Google Play emulators. Switch to one without it:

  1. Open Device Manager
  2. Click Create Device
  3. Select Pixel 4 → Next
  4. On System Image screen — pick an image that shows:
    • ✅ No Google Play icon in the Play Store column
    • Example: API 30, Google APIs (NOT Google Play)
  5. Finish setup and use this emulator

Difference between emulator types:

Emulator TypeIconInstall Debug APK
Google Play🔺 Triangle❌ Blocks it
Google APIsNo triangle✅ Allows it
No GoogleNo triangle✅ Allows it

Fix 4 — Enable Developer Options on Emulator

On the emulator:

  1. Go to Settings → About Emulated Device
  2. Tap Build Number 7 times
  3. Go back to Settings → Developer Options
  4. Make sure "USB Debugging" is ON
  5. Also turn ON "Allow ADB debugging in charge only mode" if visible

Recommended: Fix 3 is the permanent solution — create a Google APIs emulator (without Play Store) and you will never get this error again. Debug APKs install freely on those. ✅

remainder , alarm and all not working everything is fine

Mar 30

The app installs but reminders/alarms don't fire. This is a common issue on emulators. Let me fix it step by step.

Fix 1 — Replace AlarmScheduler.kt completely

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/utils/AlarmScheduler.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.utils

import android.app.AlarmManager
import android.app.PendingIntent
import android.content.Context
import android.content.Intent
import android.os.Build
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.model.Medicine
import com.example.medicinereminder.receiver.AlarmReceiver
import java.util.Calendar

object AlarmScheduler {

    fun scheduleAlarm(context: Context, medicine: Medicine) {
        if (!medicine.isEnabled) return

        val alarmManager = context.getSystemService(Context.ALARM_SERVICE) as AlarmManager

        val intent = Intent(context, AlarmReceiver::class.java).apply {
            putExtra("medicine_id", medicine.id)
            putExtra("medicine_name", medicine.name)
        }

        val pendingIntent = PendingIntent.getBroadcast(
            context,
            medicine.id,
            intent,
            PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT or PendingIntent.FLAG_IMMUTABLE
        )

        val calendar = Calendar.getInstance().apply {
            set(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY, medicine.hour)
            set(Calendar.MINUTE, medicine.minute)
            set(Calendar.SECOND, 0)
            set(Calendar.MILLISECOND, 0)
            if (timeInMillis <= System.currentTimeMillis()) {
                add(Calendar.DAY_OF_YEAR, 1)
            }
        }

        try {
            when {
                Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.S -> {
                    if (alarmManager.canScheduleExactAlarms()) {
                        alarmManager.setExactAndAllowWhileIdle(
                            AlarmManager.RTC_WAKEUP,
                            calendar.timeInMillis,
                            pendingIntent
                        )
                    } else {
                        // Fallback if exact alarm permission not granted
                        alarmManager.setAndAllowWhileIdle(
                            AlarmManager.RTC_WAKEUP,
                            calendar.timeInMillis,
                            pendingIntent
                        )
                    }
                }
                Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.M -> {
                    alarmManager.setExactAndAllowWhileIdle(
                        AlarmManager.RTC_WAKEUP,
                        calendar.timeInMillis,
                        pendingIntent
                    )
                }
                else -> {
                    alarmManager.setExact(
                        AlarmManager.RTC_WAKEUP,
                        calendar.timeInMillis,
                        pendingIntent
                    )
                }
            }
        } catch (e: Exception) {
            e.printStackTrace()
        }
    }

    fun cancelAlarm(context: Context, medicine: Medicine) {
        val alarmManager = context.getSystemService(Context.ALARM_SERVICE) as AlarmManager
        val intent = Intent(context, AlarmReceiver::class.java)
        val pendingIntent = PendingIntent.getBroadcast(
            context,
            medicine.id,
            intent,
            PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT or PendingIntent.FLAG_IMMUTABLE
        )
        alarmManager.cancel(pendingIntent)
    }
}

Fix 2 — Replace NotificationHelper.kt completely

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/utils/NotificationHelper.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.utils

import android.app.NotificationChannel
import android.app.NotificationManager
import android.app.PendingIntent
import android.content.Context
import android.content.Intent
import android.os.Build
import androidx.core.app.NotificationCompat
import androidx.core.app.NotificationManagerCompat
import com.example.medicinereminder.R
import com.example.medicinereminder.ui.activities.MainActivity

object NotificationHelper {

    const val CHANNEL_ID = "medicine_reminder_channel"

    fun createNotificationChannel(context: Context) {
        if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.O) {
            val channel = NotificationChannel(
                CHANNEL_ID,
                "Medicine Reminders",
                NotificationManager.IMPORTANCE_HIGH
            ).apply {
                description = "Reminds you to take your medicine"
                enableVibration(true)
                enableLights(true)
            }
            val manager = context.getSystemService(Context.NOTIFICATION_SERVICE)
                    as NotificationManager
            manager.createNotificationChannel(channel)
        }
    }

    fun showNotification(context: Context, medicineId: Int, medicineName: String) {
        // Create channel first
        createNotificationChannel(context)

        val intent = Intent(context, MainActivity::class.java).apply {
            flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
        }

        val pendingIntent = PendingIntent.getActivity(
            context,
            medicineId,
            intent,
            PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT or PendingIntent.FLAG_IMMUTABLE
        )

        val notification = NotificationCompat.Builder(context, CHANNEL_ID)
            .setSmallIcon(R.drawable.ic_notification)
            .setContentTitle("Time to take your medicine!")
            .setContentText("$medicineName - Don't forget!")
            .setPriority(NotificationCompat.PRIORITY_MAX)
            .setCategory(NotificationCompat.CATEGORY_ALARM)
            .setAutoCancel(true)
            .setContentIntent(pendingIntent)
            .setVibrate(longArrayOf(0, 500, 200, 500))
            .setDefaults(NotificationCompat.DEFAULT_ALL)
            .build()

        try {
            val manager = context.getSystemService(Context.NOTIFICATION_SERVICE)
                    as NotificationManager
            manager.notify(medicineId, notification)
        } catch (e: SecurityException) {
            e.printStackTrace()
        }
    }
}

Fix 3 — Replace AndroidManifest.xml completely

Path: app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml

xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android">

    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETED" />
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM" />
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.USE_EXACT_ALARM" />
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS" />
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.VIBRATE" />
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.WAKE_LOCK" />
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE" />

    <application
        android:allowBackup="true"
        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
        android:label="@string/app_name"
        android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
        android:supportsRtl="true"
        android:theme="@style/Theme.MedicineReminder">

        <activity
            android:name=".ui.activities.LoginActivity"
            android:exported="true">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>

        <activity
            android:name=".ui.activities.RegisterActivity"
            android:exported="false" />

        <activity
            android:name=".ui.activities.MainActivity"
            android:exported="false" />

        <activity
            android:name=".ui.activities.AddMedicineActivity"
            android:exported="false" />

        <receiver
            android:name=".receiver.AlarmReceiver"
            android:exported="false" />

        <receiver
            android:name=".receiver.BootReceiver"
            android:exported="true">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.BOOT_COMPLETED" />
            </intent-filter>
        </receiver>

    </application>

</manifest>

Fix 4 — Replace MainActivity.kt completely

Path: app/src/main/java/com/example/medicinereminder/ui/activities/MainActivity.kt

kotlin
package com.example.medicinereminder.ui.activities

import android.Manifest
import android.content.Intent
import android.content.pm.PackageManager
import android.os.Build
import android.os.Bundle
import android.widget.Toast
import androidx.activity.result.contract.ActivityResultContracts
import androidx.activity.viewModels
import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity
import androidx.core.content.ContextCompat
import androidx.recyclerview.widget.LinearLayoutManager
import com.example.medicinereminder.databinding.ActivityMainBinding
import com.example.medicinereminder.ui.adapters.MedicineAdapter
import com.example.medicinereminder.ui.viewmodel.MedicineViewModel
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.AlarmScheduler
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.NotificationHelper
import com.example.medicinereminder.utils.SharedPrefsHelper

class MainActivity : AppCompatActivity() {

    private lateinit var binding: ActivityMainBinding
    private val viewModel: MedicineViewModel by viewModels()
    private lateinit var adapter: MedicineAdapter

    private val notificationPermissionLauncher = registerForActivityResult(
        ActivityResultContracts.RequestPermission()
    ) { isGranted ->
        if (isGranted) {
            Toast.makeText(this, "Notifications enabled!", Toast.LENGTH_SHORT).show()
        } else {
            Toast.makeText(
                this,
                "Please enable notifications in Settings for reminders to work",
                Toast.LENGTH_LONG
            ).show()
        }
    }

    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
        binding = ActivityMainBinding.inflate(layoutInflater)
        setContentView(binding.root)

        // Create notification channel immediately
        NotificationHelper.createNotificationChannel(this)

        // Request notification permission on Android 13+
        requestNotificationPermission()

        val username = SharedPrefsHelper.getUsername(this)
        supportActionBar?.subtitle = "Hello, $username"

        setupRecyclerView()
        observeMedicines()

        binding.fabAdd.setOnClickListener {
            startActivity(Intent(this, AddMedicineActivity::class.java))
        }

        binding.btnLogout.setOnClickListener {
            SharedPrefsHelper.logout(this)
            startActivity(Intent(this, LoginActivity::class.java))
            finish()
        }
    }

    private fun setupRecyclerView() {
        adapter = MedicineAdapter(
            onDeleteClick = { medicine ->
                AlertDialog.Builder(this)
                    .setTitle("Delete Medicine")
                    .setMessage("Delete ${medicine.name}?")
                    .setPositiveButton("Delete") { _, _ ->
                        AlarmScheduler.cancelAlarm(this, medicine)
                        viewModel.delete(medicine)
                        Toast.makeText(
                            this,
                            "${medicine.name} deleted",
                            Toast.LENGTH_SHORT
                        ).show()
                    }
                    .setNegativeButton("Cancel", null)
                    .show()
            },
            onToggleClick = { medicine, isEnabled ->
                val updated = medicine.copy(isEnabled = isEnabled)
                viewModel.update(updated)
                if (isEnabled) {
                    AlarmScheduler.scheduleAlarm(this, updated)
                    Toast.makeText(
                        this,
                        "${medicine.name} reminder ON",
                        Toast.LENGTH_SHORT
                    ).show()
                } else {
                    AlarmScheduler.cancelAlarm(this, updated)
                    Toast.makeText(
                        this,
                        "${medicine.name} reminder OFF",
                        Toast.LENGTH_SHORT
                    ).show()
                }
            }
        )

        binding.recyclerView.apply {
            layoutManager = LinearLayoutManager(this@MainActivity)
            adapter = this@MainActivity.adapter
            setHasFixedSize(true)
        }
    }

    private fun observeMedicines() {
        viewModel.allMedicines.observe(this) { medicines ->
            adapter.submitList(medicines)
            if (medicines.isEmpty()) {
                binding.tvEmpty.visibility = android.view.View.VISIBLE
                binding.recyclerView.visibility = android.view.View.GONE
            } else {
                binding.tvEmpty.visibility = android.view.View.GONE
                binding.recyclerView.visibility = android.view.View.VISIBLE
            }
        }
    }

    private fun requestNotificationPermission() {
        if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.TIRAMISU) {
            when {
                ContextCompat.checkSelfPermission(
                    this,
                    Manifest.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS
                ) == PackageManager.PERMISSION_GRANTED -> {
                    // Already granted
                }
                else -> {
                    notificationPermissionLauncher.launch(
                        Manifest.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS
                    )
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

Fix 5 — Test alarm quickly on emulator

To test without waiting, set alarm 2 minutes from now:

  1. Add a medicine
  2. Set time to current time + 2 minutes
  3. Save
  4. Leave the app open or minimize it
  5. Wait 2 minutes — notification should appear

Why alarms were not working — 3 reasons:

ReasonFix Applied
Used setRepeating() — inexact on API 19+Changed to setExactAndAllowWhileIdle()
Notification permission not properly handledAdded proper permission flow
Missing DEFAULT_ALL flag on notificationAdded — enables sound + vibration

After replacing all files → Build → Clean → Run ✅

package com.example.medicinereminder.ui.adapters
6
 
7
import android.view.LayoutInflater
8
import android.view.ViewGroup
9
import androidx.recyclerview.widget.DiffUtil
10
import androidx.recyclerview.widget.ListAdapter
11
import androidx.recyclerview.widget.RecyclerView
12
import com.example.medicinereminder.data.model.Medicine
13
import com.example.medicinereminder.databinding.ItemCalendarMedicineBinding
14
import java.util.Locale
15
 
16
/**
17
* CalendarMedicineAdapter: shows medicines in the calendar view.
18
* Displays name, time, and repeat info.
19
*/
20
class CalendarMedicineAdapter :
21
ListAdapter<Medicine, CalendarMedicineAdapter.ViewHolder>(DiffCallback()) {
22
 
23
override fun onCreateViewHolder(parent: ViewGroup, viewType: Int): ViewHolder {
24
val binding = ItemCalendarMedicineBinding.inflate(
25
LayoutInflater.from(parent.context), parent, false
26
)
27
return ViewHolder(binding)
28
}
29
 
30
override fun onBindViewHolder(holder: ViewHolder, position: Int) {
31
holder.bind(getItem(position))
32
}
33
 
34
inner class ViewHolder(private val binding: ItemCalendarMedicineBinding) :
35
RecyclerView.ViewHolder(binding.root) {
36
 
37
fun bind(medicine: Medicine) {
38
binding.tvCalMedicineName.text = medicine.name
39
binding.tvCalMedicineTime.text = String.format(
40
Locale.getDefault(), "%02d:%02d", medicine.hour, medicine.minute
41
)
42
binding.tvCalRepeat.text = "Daily"
43
binding.tvCalStatus.text = if (medicine.isEnabled) "Active" else "Disabled"
44
binding.tvCalStatus.setTextColor(
45
if (medicine.isEnabled)
46
binding.root.context.getColor(android.R.color.holo_green_dark)
47
else
48
binding.root.context.getColor(android.R.color.holo_red_dark)
49
)
50
}
51
}
52
 
53
class DiffCallback : DiffUtil.ItemCallback<Medicine>() {
54
override fun areItemsTheSame(oldItem: Medicine, newItem: Medicine) =
55
oldItem.id == newItem.id
56
override fun areContentsTheSame(oldItem: Medicine, newItem: Medicine) =
57
oldItem == newItem
58
}
59
}
60
